title: FuelCell Energy Announces Global Restructuring Plan
text: FuelCell Energy announced global restructuring of operations in US, Canada, and Germany aiming to reduce operating costs by approximately 15% in FY2025. Plan includes 17% workforce reduction (including September 2024 actions). Company reducing spending on product development, overhead, and operational expenses. Strategic focus areas: (1) Distributed Power Generation - expanding Connecticut-manufactured molten carbonate technology to deliver power solutions addressing grid deficiencies and supporting rapid data center expansion. (2) Grid Resiliency - meeting increasing power demands from AI-driven data center growth, leveraging distributed energy advantages avoiding lengthy permitting delays. (3) Carbon Capture & Recovery - continuing CO2 recovery initiatives for food/beverage and industrial, including joint carbon capture platform demonstration at Port of Rotterdam. Additional elements: refining solid oxide technology for electrolysis and power generation, pursuing multi-megawatt electrolyzer partnerships, planning solid oxide electrolyzer demonstration at Idaho National Laboratory in 2025, expanding presence in South Korean market. Manufacturing preserved: carbonate manufacturing at Torrington, Connecticut facility expected to operate at or above FY2024 levels. Restructuring aims to protect competitive position amid slower-than-expected clean energy investments.
